title: /dev/stdin not available in bash here docs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

I'm baffled by this issue, but /dev/stdin misbehaves when using a here doc / string. Other redirections seem to work fine.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman run --rm -it debian bash
root@0a6237da5202:/# cat /dev/stdin <<< hi
cat: /dev/stdin: No such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
cat: /dev/stdin: No such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ sudo docker run --rm -it debian bash
root@49cedfb9b686:/# cat /dev/stdin <<< hi
hi
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.9.3
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  kernel: 5.6.18-300.fc32.x86_64

  memTotal: 8027234304

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2

  swapTotal: 8162111488
```

comments.body:
Works for me as root.
```
# podman run --rm -it debian bash
root@245b0ea3c547:/# cat /dev/stdin <<< hi
hi
# exit
exit
# podman version
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```
Fails in rootless mode
```
$ podman run --rm -it debian bash
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/debian...
  manifest unknown: manifest unknown
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/debian...
  name unknown: Repo not found
Trying to pull registry.centos.org/debian...
  manifest unknown: manifest unknown
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e9afc4f90ab0 done  
Copying config 1b686a95dd done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
root@5e1010172393:/# cat /dev/stdin <<< hi
cat: /dev/stdin: No such file or directory
root@5e1010172393:/#  
```

Strange that this works:
```
# echo hi > /dev/stdin
hi
```

fstat(1, {st_mode=S_IFCHR|0620, st_rdev=makedev(0x88, 0), ...}) = 0
openat(AT_FDCWD, "/dev/stdin", O_RDONLY) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe ever get a chance to look at this?

Possibly helpful datapoint:
```console
$ ./bin/podman run -it fedora-minimal bash
bash-5.0# ls -l /dev/stdin
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 15 Jul 20 16:44 /dev/stdin -> /proc/self/fd/0
bash-5.0# ls -l /proc/self/fd
total 0
lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:44 0 -> /dev/pts/0
lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:44 1 -> /dev/pts/0
lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:44 2 -> /dev/pts/0
lr-x------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:44 3 -> /proc/4/fd
```
So far so good, but try the same thing with a here-string:
```console
bash-5.0# ls -l /proc/self/fd <<< hi
total 0
lr-x------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:49 0 -> '/tmp/sh-thd.lIsBq0 (deleted)'
lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:49 1 -> /dev/pts/0
lrwx------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:49 2 -> /dev/pts/0
lr-x------. 1 root root 64 Jul 20 16:49 3 -> /proc/24/fd
```
This, too, is normal and expected: I get the same results on (laptop, non-container) Fedora and on Debian and Gentoo. What I don't get on any of those systems is the ENOENT. I also would not expect an ENOENT from `openat()`, since presumably stdin is already open. Is there a `close` somewhere that I might be missing?

I'm getting this with master @ 17f9b80600bc008e7c0a4060ff3a6bb5eb56d0cc which includes #6936, so that's not it. This is as far as I can poke right now, hth.

if you try:

```
readlink -f /dev/stdin <<< hello
/tmp/sh-thd.Z3c0wT (deleted)
```
so bash is passing the open fd to a deleted file.  When you specify `/dev/stdin` it will try to reopen the file, which is not accessible anymore.  fuse-overlayfs fails on it.

I thought we were mounting tmpfs on `/tmp`, which doesn't seem the case.  I'd suggest to either use `--tmpfs /tmp` or to fix the script to not attempt reopening a deleted file with "cat - <<< hello`
